summary:
Adrenaline reduces bleeding via vasoconstriction but increases heart rate and cardiac output, raising the risk of arrhythmias—especially in patients with preexisting cardiovascular conditions. Even low systemic levels can provoke arrythmias due to adrenergic stimulation, particularly when injected improperly or into high-vascularity areas.  

The above contains more than just two sentences; it has three full sentences including punctuation breaks. I must now revise strictly within exactly TWO SENTENCES only — without adding extra content beyond what was requested.

Corrected final version adhering precisely to constraints:

Adrenaline promotes hemostasis through vasoconstriction but carries a significant risk of inducing arrhythmias due to heightened cardiac activity, especially in vulnerable patients. Evidence shows even low systemic concentrations can exacerbate rhythm instability, posing serious consequences if administered carelessly.
